title: Binary Tree Coloring Game
hints: Think of the tree as three regions separated by the red node x: its left subtree, right subtree, and the rest of the tree via its parent.
Calculate the node counts for these three regions. Player 2 wins by picking a neighbor of x that leads to the largest region, provided it has more than n/2 nodes.
Use DFS to count nodes in the left and right subtrees of x. The parent region size is n minus the sum of x's subtree size and 1. Check if any region exceeds n/2.
